summary: Zoning board: Effect.tapBoth does not exist (v4.0.0-beta.66) — plan adapted to Effect.tap + Effect.tapError chained
detail: tapBoth exists on Stream but not on Effect in this version. The plan uses Effect.tap (success) + Effect.tapError (failure) chained, each wrapped in catchAll. This is functionally equivalent and follows Effect v4 API conventions.

summary: SHOWSTOPPER: appendQueues in filesystem.ts:176-186 permanently poisons all future writes to same file after single I/O failure — blocks reliable telemetry
detail: The `appendQueues` Map chains promises: `prev.then(() => NFS.appendFile(...))`. When `appendFile` rejects (disk full, permission denied), the rejected promise stays as the queue tail. All subsequent `appendLine` calls chain `.then()` onto a rejected promise — the write callback never executes and the rejection propagates. Effect: after one transient I/O error, ALL future JSONL writes to that file fail silently for the process lifetime. Affects ALL 26+ callers of appendLine (bash_usage, smart_tool_usage, feedback, findings, lessons, etc.). Fix: delete the Map entry on rejection so the next write reinitializes from Promise.resolve(). This is a prerequisite for reliable tool invocation telemetry.

summary: Tool execution has 3 paths — wrap() covers builtin (~80 tools) but plugin/MCP bypass it entirely via registry.ts:fromPlugin() and session/tools.ts:139-200
detail: Foundation subagent confirmed: builtin tools use wrap() at tool.ts:144-253. Plugin tools use fromPlugin() at registry.ts:328-390 which constructs Tool.Def with its own execute (Effect.gen → Effect.promise → Effect.catch → Effect.withSpan). MCP tools use session/tools.ts:139-200 with a third independent pipeline. The true universal convergence is session/tools.ts:resolve() but instrumenting there is a larger change. The user's directive is 'smallest possible change' — wrap()-only instrumentation covers ~80 tools. Plugin and MCP coverage is deferred to a future lane.
